Title: Leaf herbivory counteracts nematode-triggered repression of jasmonate-related defenses in tomato roots

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to form 13-hydroperoxy fatty acid intermediates that initiate jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with resistance to herbivores and nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf herbivory systemically induces LOXD expression in roots and counteracts Meloidogyne incognita-mediated repression, associated with higher root OPDA/JA and reduced nematode performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of root transcripts; root phytohormone profiling; herbivory/nematode bioassays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression induced by Manduca sexta leaf herbivory; repressed at early infection stages by Meloidogyne incognita; additive induction under dual attack at 3 d
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOXD was downregulated in roots during early M. incognita infection. 2. Manduca sexta leaf herbivory systemically upregulated LOXD in roots. 3. Under dual attack, herbivory counteracted nematode-induced repression of the 13-LOX branch, coinciding with reduced galling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita; 3, 7, and 21 d after inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point suggested for jasmonate-related root defense, not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to strengthen root jasmonate-related defense and anti-nematode resistance, though constitutive activation may alter defense-growth balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a marker gene for the 13-LOX branch; direct causal genetic manipulation of LOXD was not performed in this study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 13-hydroperoxy fatty acid intermediates into unstable allene oxide intermediates in jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with resistance to herbivores and nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Systemically induced by leaf herbivory and repressed by nematode infection in roots; herbivory antagonizes repression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression activated by Manduca sexta herbivory and reduced by Meloidogyne incognita at early stages
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS1 expression was suppressed by early M. incognita infection. 2. Shoot herbivory activated AOS1 expression in roots. 3. Coinfestation restored expression relative to nematode-only roots.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to maintain jasmonate biosynthesis under nematode attack
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene; no direct mutant analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 13-hydroperoxy fatty acids to allene oxide intermediates in the jasmonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with resistance to herbivores and nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf herbivory induces AOS2 expression in roots and offsets suppression by M. incognita
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots by Manduca sexta; reduced by early nematode infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS2 is part of the 13-LOX branch repressed by M. incognita in roots. 2. Leaf herbivory systemically activates AOS2. 3. Dual challenge results in expression intermediate between single treatments, opposing nematode repression.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for preserving root oxylipin defense under nematode pressure
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene; no direct mutant analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes unstable allene oxide to form OPDA in jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with resistance to herbivores and nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOC root expression is activated by leaf herbivory and repressed by nematodes early; dual attack counteracts repression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression increased by Manduca sexta feeding; decreased by early M. incognita infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC expression decreases during early M. incognita infection. 2. Shoot herbivory upregulates AOC in roots. 3. This response coincides with increased root OPDA/JA and reduced nematode success.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway engineering target to reinforce OPDA/JA production in roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene only in this study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces OPDA in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway leading toward jasmonic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with resistance to herbivores and nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root OPR3 expression is systemically induced by leaf herbivory and repressed by M. incognita early infection; herbivory reverses repression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated in roots by leaf herbivory; downregulated in roots during early nematode infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 expression is transiently repressed by M. incognita in roots. 2. Manduca sexta herbivory induces OPR3 systemically. 3. In coinfested plants, OPR3 expression is higher than in nematode-only plants, matching stronger jasmonate-related defense.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to sustain JA output during nematode attack
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene; no gene-specific functional perturbation in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to generate 9-hydroperoxy fatty acid intermediates for 9-LOX-derived oxylipins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Putative root defense metabolites associated with resistance signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf herbivory generally increases LOXA root expression; nematode infection alone has little effect except induction at 21 d
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression increased by Manduca sexta over the experiment; additive induction under dual attack at 21 d
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOXA was not generally repressed by M. incognita. 2. Manduca sexta leaf herbivory systemically induced LOXA in roots. 3. Coinfestation produced an additive increase at 21 d.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance 9-LOX-associated root defense, though precise metabolite outputs remain unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study suggests involvement of 9-LOX branch in systemic root defense, but specific products were not measured

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 9-hydroperoxy fatty acid substrates to allene oxide intermediates in the 9-LOX branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Systemically induced in roots by leaf herbivory; not significantly altered by nematode infection overall
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression upregulated by Manduca sexta throughout the experiment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS3 expression was generally unaffected by M. incognita. 2. Leaf herbivory increased AOS3 root expression. 3. Coinfested plants resembled herbivory-only plants for AOS3 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for modulating 9-LOX root defenses if validated genetically
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific downstream metabolites were not determined in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxides into divinyl ether oxylipins in the 9-LOX branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Putative defense compounds implicated in root defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DES is generally induced by leaf herbivory at later times in roots; nematode infection has little overall effect
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by herbivory at 3 d but upregulated at 7 and 21 d; in coinfested plants expression similar to herbivory-only plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DES was not significantly affected overall by M. incognita. 2. Leaf herbivory caused time-dependent DES regulation, with induction at 7 and 21 d. 3. Coinfested roots retained herbivory-associated DES expression patterns.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for engineering 9-LOX-derived defense chemistry, but metabolic outputs require confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses possible defensive roles of 9-LOX products but does not directly quantify DES products

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: jasmonic acid-regulated root defense metabolites; jasmonate-related oxylipins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against root-knot nematodes and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Shoot jasmonate perception controlling systemic induction of root defense metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: jasmonate perception/signaling required for systemic activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for leaf herbivory-triggered systemic activation of root jasmonate-related defenses
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Root 13-LOX pathway and downstream jasmonate-regulated defense metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential shoot-side regulator for the systemic anti-nematode effect of leaf herbivory
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of shoot jasmonate perception in jai1 scions abolishes the Ma. sexta-induced reduction in root galls and can even increase galling under herbivory
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Grafting with mutant scions; herbivory/nematode bioassays; root JA and JA-Ile measurements in grafts
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; jasmonate signal
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely jasmonate receptor complex, not directly tested in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf herbivory by Manduca sexta; root infection by Meloidogyne incognita; grafted plants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered baseline root galling in graft combinations with mutant scions indicates broader effects on susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining intact shoot jasmonate perception is important for systemic anti-nematode defense; avoid disrupting JAI1 signaling in breeding for multitrophic resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This was the only gene with direct functional genetics demonstrating necessity for the shoot-to-root interaction in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Shoot jasmonate perception, tested with jai1 scions, is required for Manduca sexta herbivory to reduce Meloidogyne incognita galling. 2. In jai1/wt grafts, herbivory failed to suppress and even increased root galling. 3. Therefore shoot jasmonate perception is decisive for systemic activation of anti-nematode root defenses.
